subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Differential Equations (Draining Barrel)
stimulus: A cylindrical barrel with a diameter of 2 feet contains collected rainwater, as shown in the figure above. The water drains out through a valve (not shown) at the bottom of the barrel. The rate of change of the height $h$ of the water in the barrel with respect to time $t$ is modeled by $\dfrac{dh}{dt} = -\dfrac{1}{10}\sqrt{h}$, where $h$ is measured in feet and $t$ is measured in seconds. (The volume $V$ of a cylinder with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \pi r^2 h$.)

parts.prompt: Find the rate of change of the volume of water in the barrel with respect to time when the height of the water is 4 feet. Indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: Correct setup using $V = \pi r^2 h = \pi(1)^2 h = \pi h$ (since radius = 1 ft), so $\dfrac{dV}{dt} = \pi \dfrac{dh}{dt}$
Correct answer with units: when $h = 4$, $\dfrac{dV}{dt} = \pi\left(-\dfrac{1}{10}\sqrt{4}\right) = -\dfrac{\pi}{5}$ cubic feet per second

parts.prompt: When the height of the water is 3 feet, is the rate of change of the height of the water with respect to time increasing or decreasing? Explain your reasoning.

scoring_points.description: Correct computation of $\dfrac{d^2h}{dt^2}$ by differentiating $\dfrac{dh}{dt} = -\dfrac{1}{10}h^{1/2}$ with respect to $t$: $\dfrac{d^2h}{dt^2} = -\dfrac{1}{10}\cdot\dfrac{1}{2}h^{-1/2}\dfrac{dh}{dt}$
Correct conclusion: since $\dfrac{d^2h}{dt^2} > 0$, the rate of change of the height is increasing at $h = 3$ feet

parts.prompt: At time $t = 0$ seconds, the height of the water is 5 feet. Use separation of variables to find an expression for $h$ in terms of $t$.
parts.scoring_notes: Note: 0/4 if no separation of variables. Note: max 2/4 [1-1-0-0] if no constant of integration.

scoring_points.description: Correct antidifferentiation of both sides: $2\sqrt{h} = -\dfrac{t}{10} + C$
Correct application of initial condition $h(0) = 5$: $2\sqrt{5} = C$, so $2\sqrt{h} = -\dfrac{t}{10} + 2\sqrt{5}$
Correct explicit solution for $h$: $h(t) = \left(\sqrt{5} - \dfrac{t}{20}\right)^2$ for $0 \leq t \leq 20\sqrt{5}$
